title: What to know about California’s messy governor’s race
description: Its unusual primary system, combined with scandal and a low-profile field, have made this year’s race harder to predict.

title: Who’s leading the wide-open 2028 Democratic presidential field?
description: The early field is still wide open.

title: One by one, U.S. civil rights agency dismantles tools to fight discrimination
description: The EEOC is seeking to overturn rules created decades ago to tackle discrimination in employment. The Trump administration says those rules have led to more discrimination —against white people.

title: Likely Democratic nominee Graham Platner talks about Maine's upcoming Senate race
description: NPR's Leila Fadel asks likely Democratic nominee Graham Platner how he plans to beat incumbent Republican Susan Collins in Maine's Senate race.

title: After musicians drop out of Freedom 250, Trump floats rally featuring him
description: Over the weekend, President Trump suggested an event celebrating America's 250th birthday should instead be a rally with him as the headliner after many of the artists slated to perform dropped out.

title: After Memorial Day break, Congress returns to D.C. with long to-do list
description: Congress returns to Washington with a long to-do list, including disentangling immigration enforcement funding from the President's weaponization fund.

title: Sen. Cory Booker on Newark ICE detention center protest and Congress' return to D.C.
description: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks Democratic Sen. Cory Booker about protests around an ICE detention facility in his home state of New Jersey and lawmakers' agenda as Congress returns to Washington.

title: The midterms are just months away. What message is the GOP sharing to sway voters?
description: With five months to go until the general election, how are Republicans running for office explaining to voters why they should retain control of Congress?

title: After a Memorial Day break, Congress returns to the same problems they left behind
description: Congress returns to town with the same problems they left before Memorial Day: immigration funding, opposition to a new Justice Department fund, and questions about Iran war oversight.

title: How aid cuts are hampering the frontline response to the Ebola crisis
description: Aid workers in Uganda are watching the Ebola crisis unfold in the neighboring Democratic Republic of Congo. They're doing what they can to prepare for an uptick in cases, but foreign aid cuts aren't helping.

title: Trump floats MAGA rally instead of concert after musicians drop out of Freedom 250
description: Several artists, including country singer Martina McBride, have withdrawn from the Great American State Fair in Washington, D.C.

title: Spanberger’s veto spree turns many in her own party against her
description: The Virginia governor is being attacked from all sides for actions she considers moderate, including 31 vetoes of bills from the Democratic-led legislature.

title: U.S. strike on alleged drug boat kills 3 in Pacific Ocean, in fourth attack this week
description: The U.S. military said it carried out another strike on a boat accused of smuggling drugs in the Pacific Ocean, killing three in the fourth attack this week and putting the total death toll at 205.

title: Why the favorite for California governor has divided his Biden-era colleagues
description: Xavier Becerra led President Joe Biden’s health agency during the pandemic but kept a low profile. He has risen to lead in polls ahead of California’s gubernatorial primary.

title: Blue states pitch 100 percent tax on Trump’s ‘anti-weaponization’ payouts
description: “If you storm the Capitol and you take from this slush fund, too bad, we’re taking it,” one New York lawmaker said.

title: Trump’s doctor says he is in ‘excellent health’ after latest checkup
description: While the president’s medical report says he remains fit to serve, independent physicians have raised questions about the recurrent bruises on his hands and swelling in his legs.
